codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly removes hard preflight rejection for empty companies when intent is non-blank (VAL-API-010), preserves blank-intent guardrail under unrestricted scope (VAL-API-011), and enables browser_only runs with empty companies to execute grounded_web without legacy fixed-company defaults (VAL-ROUTE-007). All 35 webhook tests pass. Typecheck and lint pass.

issues.description: VAL-ROUTE-007 behavior (unrestricted grounded execution without legacy fixed-company defaults) is implemented via `companiesToSearch = [{ name: "" }]` but not directly unit-tested in isolation. The mock in VAL-API-010 test doesn't verify the actual company-transformed config passed to runDiscovery. This is acceptable since integration tests would exercise this path, but a dedicated unit test for `runGroundedWebDiscovery` with empty companies would improve coverage.

summary: Feature passes scrutiny. The implementation correctly addresses all three assertions: (1) removes hard 409 preflight rejection when companies are empty but intent is non-blank, (2) preserves explicit 400 blank-intent rejection under unrestricted scope, and (3) enables browser_only runs with empty companies to execute grounded_web without injecting legacy defaults (Scale AI/Figma/Notion). Tests pass and implementation follows existing patterns.
